samples.xml_description:
<module id="adder_32bit">
    <description>Implement a carry-lookahead 32-bit adder.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="S" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit output representing the sum of A and B.</output>
        <output id="C32">Carry-out output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter>Carry</parameter>
        <parameter>Carry lookahead logic is used to calculate the carry outputs based on the generate and propagate signals.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    The carry-lookahead adder is a fast arithmetic unit that performs addition of two 32-bit numbers A and B. The adder computes the sum S and carry-out C32 outputs by generating carry signals based on the generate and propagate signals of each bit.
    The adder consists of several blocks that compute the generate and propagate signals for groups of bits, and use those signals to compute the carry signals. The carry signals are then used to compute the final sum output S.
    </implementation>
</module>
